subject: Life Skills
title: Healthy Relationships
tagline: Build positive relationships and recognise peer pressure!

stages.name: Stage 1
stages.question: What makes a relationship healthy?
options: Being influenced by friends to do something
Repeated harmful behaviour towards someone
Mutual respect and trust
A trusted adult
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Mutual respect and trust is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Mutual respect and trust.

stages.name: Stage 2
stages.question: What is 'consent'?
options: Understanding and sharing another person's feelings
Repeated harmful behaviour towards someone
Controlling behaviour
Freely agreeing to something
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Freely agreeing to something is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Freely agreeing to something.

stages.name: Stage 3
stages.question: Why is communication important in relationships?
options: It helps people understand each other
They help protect your comfort and safety
Mutual respect and trust
Calmly explaining how you feel
stages.correctOption: It helps people understand each other
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! It helps people understand each other is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is It helps people understand each other.

stages.name: Stage 4
stages.question: What is a sign of an unhealthy relationship?
options: Repeated harmful behaviour towards someone
Freely agreeing to something
Calmly explaining how you feel
Controlling behaviour
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Controlling behaviour is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Controlling behaviour.

stages.name: Stage 5
stages.question: What is 'peer pressure'?
options: It builds trust between people
Honesty, support and kindness
Being influenced by friends to do something
Controlling behaviour
stages.correctOption: Being influenced by friends to do something
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Being influenced by friends to do something is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Being influenced by friends to do something.

stages.name: Stage 6
stages.question: How can you handle peer pressure?
options: Understanding and sharing another person's feelings
Finding a fair way to solve a disagreement
They help protect your comfort and safety
Confidently say no and walk away
stages.correctOption: Confidently say no and walk away
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Confidently say no and walk away is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Confidently say no and walk away.

stages.name: Stage 7
stages.question: What is empathy in a relationship?
options: Being influenced by friends to do something
Finding a fair way to solve a disagreement
Understanding and sharing another person's feelings
It builds trust between people
stages.correctOption: Understanding and sharing another person's feelings
stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Understanding and sharing another person's feelings is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Understanding and sharing another person's feelings.

stages.name: Stage 8
stages.question: Why are boundaries important?
options: They help protect your comfort and safety
It helps people understand each other
Calmly explaining how you feel
Finding a fair way to solve a disagreement

stages.correctFeedback: Yes! They help protect your comfort and safety is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is They help protect your comfort and safety.

stages.name: Stage 9
stages.question: What should you do if a friend betrays your trust?
options: Confidently say no and walk away
It builds trust between people
Talk to them honestly about how you feel
Controlling behaviour

stages.correctFeedback: Yes! Talk to them honestly about how you feel is right — onward!
stages.wrongFeedback: Not quite — the answer is Talk to them honestly about how you feel.

Repeated harmful behaviour towards someone

options: A trusted adult
